displayTitle: Straight Down or at an Angle

beats.narration: In contact testing the probe rides the part on a thin couplant film. Two techniques do most of the work: straight beam and angle beam.

payload.title: STRAIGHT & ANGLE
payload.sub: Two ways to send sound into the part.

beats.narration: Straight beam sends a longitudinal wave straight down. Watch the back wall echo — a lamination adds a new echo and steals that back wall.

payload.heading: The back wall is your witness
payload.note: NEW ECHO + LOST BACK WALL = LAMINATION

beats.narration: On a wedge, a shear wave travels the steel in a vee. Thickness times tangent gives the half skip. Mark your scan zone.

payload.heading: Angle beam: geometry you can mark out

beats.narration: Thin plate wants seventy degrees, heavy sections want forty five, and always aim square at the flaw face you expect.
So here's the rule: stay coupled, mind the back wall, and face the flaw you expect. Know your triangle numbers cold.

payload.sub: And know your triangle numbers cold.
